title: Intraoperative EEG dataset during medianus-tibialis stimulation with 8 different rates

funding_references.funder_name: Alexander S. Onassis Public Benefit Foundation
Alexander S. Onassis Public Benefit Foundation

description: This intraoperative EEG dataset comprises somatosensory evoked potentials (SEPs) recorded from 18 subjects (14 adults and 4 children) during median and tibial nerve stimulation at 8 different stimulation rates. The dataset includes raw continuous EEG recordings and processed derivatives, designed to investigate the optimization of signal-to-noise ratio in short-duration SEP recordings through systematic variation of stimulus repetition rates.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded continuously during median and tibial nerve stimulation at varying rates. Offline processing included high-pass filtering at 200 Hz, stimulus artifact detection via local peak detection, and sweep definition with post-stimulus recording lengths of 50 ms for median SEP and 100 ms for tibial SEP. Data were resampled to 1200 Hz, and sweeps with amplitude exceeding 10 µV were excluded as artifact-ridden.
